But, you know, some amazing growth in terms of traffic and down funnel results at Deepgram, you know, just as a testament that this was like real impact.

You know, we saw a lot of early great success with the Reddits and the Bramps of the world and some other, you know, more early stage-based websites, and that just gave us a lot of conviction that we had something here.
And the way we thought about the agency in the early days is just we don't really view ourselves as a consistent agency.

We really want to take some of the learnings and frustrations we had from working with the agencies that had this, like, limited scope of helping us with the channel, but not all the things associated to really, like, making the foundation of that channel work.

In terms of how we think about our engagements, perhaps a bit related to, you know, the questions you're asking here, is we see no shortage of opportunities where we can apply our workflows, customize our workflows, have, you know, tested a variety of different things in the past year.
But what we find is what our tweet spot is in any engagement is first starting with content.
And there's really two key buckets in how we think about content.
There is the editorial layer that is more longer form based content and.
All the refreshes associated needed to making that content, you know, just highly enriched and effective.
It's never just like you publish something and it's done.
We can always continually enhance that content.
On the programmatic side, that tends to be more template-based.
You know, we are working with a lot of different brands where they just have a unique opportunity to scale content that doesn't really skew editorial.

So, that, that is something that is a bit more programmatic-based, a highly complex example of one, but also sometimes it's

How can we create content that is amazing answers to those questions?

from the ground up and into a massive program, was leading SEO at TripAdvisor, big up here, another managing director who, this is like the key strategy layer of our engagements, he helped build G2 in the early days, and then we have managing editors, who's a key layer of quality to any engagement, making sure it's repeatable, Sydney here was the lead editor at Semrush, so all content at Semrush went through Sydney, so anyways, point being is just, we've been able to build a really amazing team, off the back of our approach here, that just enables us to move a lot differently than multiple agency would.

I'm just curious.

sense for them.

Now, down to how quickly we can find impact.
I know you all are very early in your journey.
I can share one example of what that can look like.
From the early traffic signal standpoint, this is a brand that we are working with.
Tiro, very different space.
But why I think it is relevant is they have very low domain authority, which is like a ranking signal of quality that just, you know, based off things like backlinks.
They have no blog content.
And so we started working with them back in early January and just publishing content, you know, first week, second week of January.
And actually, this is like a pretty common, like, you won't see traffic from day one.
You'll just start to see some early signals.
so I
But here we are by like month three, four, where we're starting to get up to 100 plus clicks a day.
And that is like, from my experience, really great growth from a program of this phase with low domain authority, no blog.
To me, I just see this as a testament of the quality content that we're putting out.
And I do view that we're just like really scratching the surface for a brand like this.
So while SEO is a long game, we certainly tend to see quite consistently into month three is when we first start to see those first spikes in traffic for our engagements.
And from there, oftentimes we're going to start to get some richer signal into the subsequent months where we can start to cut that, understand it at a deeper level, what's working, what's not.
How do we double down?
How do we enrich what we're doing?
As we continue to learn topically, what is resonating, what is not, that can help us further inform the roadmap, start to think about distribution, where we're having success.

And as, you know, we get into, you know, typically around that month, five, six, that's, it's not that certainly we would, they would take us that long to drive impact down funnel, but that's tends to be when you can cut it a lot more and understand the data to help inform kind of where you're going from there.

So again, as we think about it as like a longer game, depending on how a brand is going to think about ROI, you know, we want to, you know, those that are really going to have that long vision in mind, because in our experience, when you do have that right investment and you take that right approach, the ROI more than pays it for itself in the long term.

It's all like, do you see that momentum?
Do you see that right strategy that's going to bring you to the right place into the future?
And so for us, you know, all are, I don't think we have any customer today, my knowledge that isn't expecting us to have impact down funnel.
It's not just about vanity.
So it's very much how we operate, I think.
Thank you.
That's immensely helpful.
And I know we're at time.

Totally.
Yep.

And this is how we've approached this in similar scenarios, tends to be most effective to do that through just a bit of like a 30, 40 minute call.

That would be fantastic.
Cool.

We are not an open source product.
There are open source alternatives out there.
We very much are enterprise grade.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Explore potential partnership between Runloop and GrowthX for content strategy and growth services.](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Runloop is launching next week as a coding agent platform for testing, deployment, and fine-tuning](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=226.0)
  - [GrowthX offers customized content strategies, focusing on editorial and programmatic content creation](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=607.0)
  - [GrowthX engagements typically start with a 12-month commitment, with initial results visible around month 3](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=1189.0)
  - [Runloop seeks to understand how GrowthX's services fit into their overall growth strategy and timeline](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)

## Topics

### Runloop's Current Situation and Needs

  - [Launching next week as a coding agent platform for deployment, testing, and fine-tuning](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=226.0)
  - [Target markets: coding agent companies, LLM builders, and enterprises](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=265.0)
  - [Challenges: determining TAM/ICP, differentiating in the market, and penetrating enterprise](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=350.0)
  - [Needs: mass outreach strategy, content articulation for technical product, and vertical-specific messaging](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=381.0)

### GrowthX's Approach and Services

  - [Focus on content strategy: editorial (long-form) and programmatic (template-based) content](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=835.0)
  - [Start with understanding personas and jobs-to-be-done framework](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=929.0)
  - [Aim to create high-quality, publisher-like content that answers key customer questions](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=937.0)
  - [Additional services: lead magnets, newsletters, and distribution strategies](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=835.0)
  - [Team includes former TechCrunch editor-in-chief and other industry experts](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=1063.0)

### Engagement Structure and Timeline

  - [12-month engagements with 90-day opt-out period](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=1189.0)
  - [Initial 3-4 weeks: heavy involvement for research and strategy development](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=1613.0)
  - [Ongoing: approximately 1 hour per week from the client](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=1615.0)
  - [First traffic signals typically appear around month 3](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=1261.0)
  - [Deeper insights and down-funnel impact visible around months 5-6](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=1306.0)

### ROI and Business Case

  - [Long-term approach to organic growth](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=1355.0)
  - [Early-stage results may not show immediate ROI](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=1355.0)
  - [Focus on momentum and strategy for future impact](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=1362.0)
  - [Commitment to thought leadership and content creation](https://fathom.video/share/-oKRPuAzWY9zjGiqZB9eC64w2BRxK5Wj?tab=summary&timestamp=1466.0)
